assertions.description: The destination app manifest declares migrate_from and includes a stable id.
assertions.test: (() => { const manifest = { id: '/app/', start_url: '/app/index.html', migrate_from: ['https://drive.example.com/'] }; return typeof manifest.id === 'string' && Array.isArray(manifest.migrate_from) && manifest.migrate_from[0] === 'https://drive.example.com/'; })()

assertions.description: The old origin web-app-origin-association file explicitly authorizes the destination app with allow_migration: true.
assertions.test: (() => { const destination = 'https://files.example.com/app/'; const association = { [destination]: { allow_migration: true } }; return association[destination]?.allow_migration === true; })()

assertions.description: A migrate_from claim without matching old-origin authorization is rejected by the validator.
assertions.test: (() => { const destination = 'https://files.example.com/app/'; const manifest = { id: '/app/', migrate_from: ['https://drive.example.com/'] }; const association = {}; return Array.isArray(manifest.migrate_from) && association[destination]?.allow_migration !== true; })()

assertions.description: The same-site restriction allows subdomain-to-subdomain moves under example.com and rejects example.com to example.net.
assertions.test: (() => { const site = (value) => new URL(value).hostname.split('.').slice(-2).join('.'); return site('https://drive.example.com/') === site('https://files.example.com/') && site('https://drive.example.com/') !== site('https://files.example.net/'); })()

assertions.description: The optional old-app migrate_to object carries both the destination id and a valid install_url.
assertions.test: (() => { const oldManifest = { migrate_to: { id: 'https://files.example.com/app/', install_url: 'https://files.example.com/app/install.html?from=legacy' } }; return new URL(oldManifest.migrate_to.id).origin === 'https://files.example.com' && new URL(oldManifest.migrate_to.install_url).origin === 'https://files.example.com'; })()

assertions.description: The migrate_from behavior flag accepts suggest or force, and rejects unrelated values.
assertions.test: (() => { const allowed = new Set(['suggest', 'force']); return allowed.has('suggest') && allowed.has('force') && !allowed.has('silent'); })()

assertions.description: When old routes redirect, the object form of migrate_from can provide a non-redirecting install_url for update checks.
assertions.test: (() => { const withInstall = { id: 'https://drive.example.com/', install_url: 'https://drive.example.com/install.html?noredirect=true' }; const withoutInstall = { id: 'https://drive.example.com/' }; return new URL(withInstall.install_url).origin === 'https://drive.example.com' && !('install_url' in withoutInstall); })()
